title: Ops-Operator Communication Analysis
subtitle: Ops Team Perspective  |  2026-01-29 to 2026-03-09  |  80 WhatsApp Groups

title: The Big Picture
callout: 14,143 messages across 80 WhatsApp groups in 39 days

sub_bullets: ~175 outbound text messages per day, 7 days a week

bullets: 37% of ops outbound = confirmations + ticket requests — highly templated, highly automatable
Top 4 ops members handle 37% of all outbound — 27-32 msgs/day across 20+ groups
Worst-case bookings: 12 ops messages, 0 operator replies, 4-5 ops senders chasing the same thread

title: Top Ops Team Members by Volume
callout: Top 4 members handle 37% of all outbound messages

title: #1: Confirmation Request Overload
callout: 1,495 messages (26.6% of ops outbound) — CRITICAL severity

bullets.text: Single largest category — overwhelmingly templated, identical messages
sub_bullets: ~25-50 confirmation messages per day of purely mechanical work

bullets: On one day alone, a single ops member sent 12+ identical "please confirm" messages across groups
Each requires: find the right group → type the booking ID → send formulaic message
quotes: "Dear team please confirm BW4885536" — Secil Travelier, 2026-03-09
"BW4885895 Dear team, please confirm the booking. Thank you!" — Kseniia, 2026-03-09

title: #2: Chasing Unresponsive Operators
callout: 254 explicit follow-ups + many embedded in other categories — CRITICAL severity
bullets: Worst cases: 5-12 ops messages per booking with zero operator response

bullets.text: Multiple ops members chase the same booking independently
sub_bullets: 4-5 unique senders per thread is common — no visibility into who already followed up

title: #2: Follow-Up Chain Example (BW4787613)
bullets: 4 messages from 2 ops members within 1.5 hours — all chasing the same unresponsive operator
quotes: "BW4787613 As there is no update from pax and no ticket attached, we will refund." — Thanah, 04:33
"BW4787613 hi @operator Did they reply you directly since here is no update?" — Nine09, 05:19
"Hi @operator pls help check for this booking as well." — Nine09, 05:43
"BW4787613 Okay since there is no update... We will refund shortly." — Nine09, 06:04

title: #3: Cancellation & Refund Coordination
callout: 1,158 ops messages (20.6% of outbound) — HIGH severity

bullets.text: Two distinct workflows requiring different solutions:
sub_bullets: A. Ops-initiated: customer requests relayed from Zendesk to operator
B. Operator-initiated: capacity blocks, weather, customer won't travel
Read request → Check backoffice → Process in backoffice → Confirm to operator → Update Zendesk

quotes: "ABM66312: Hi team, the customer is asking to cancel. Can we cancel and refund?" — Anastasiia
"12GO28367944 - We have closed sales for this route. Please proceed with cancellation and refund" — Porr (operator)

title: #4: Ticket Attachment Requests
callout: 659 text messages + 362 images — HIGH severity, VERY HIGH automation potential
bullets: Almost entirely identical messages: [booking_id] + "please attach the ticket"
On one day, Kseniia sent 6 word-for-word identical requests to the same group within 30 minutes
Most automatable category — no human judgment needed
quotes: "BW4887519 Dear team, please attach the ticket. Thank you!" — Kseniia, 16:18
"BW4887487 Dear team, please attach the ticket. Thank you!" — Kseniia, 16:17
"BW4887320 Dear team, please attach the ticket. Thank you!" — Kseniia, 16:14

title: #5: Multi-Group Context Switching
callout: 80 WhatsApp groups — top ops members juggle 20-33 groups daily
bullets: Constant context-switching between operators, booking IDs, and conversation threads

title: #5: Highest Ops-to-Operator Ratios (Most Chasing)
bullets: A ratio above 2.0 strongly indicates ops is chasing the operator more than being responded to

rows: Extreme — ops sends 6x more than operator replies

title: #6: Customer Complaint Relay (Zendesk → WhatsApp)

sub_bullets: Read Zendesk ticket → Extract details → Find right WhatsApp group → Compose message → Follow up

bullets: High-effort, low-frequency — messages are often 100-200 words long
quotes: "Dear Team, Booking ID: 28079373... The customer reported that they arrived 30 minutes before departure at Terminal Giwangan but could not find your bus..." — Travelier Ops 14 (208 words)

title: #7: Operator-Initiated Requests Creating Reactive Work
callout: MEDIUM severity — operators message ops with cancellations, capacity alerts, schedule changes
bullets: Each requires manual backoffice update, refund processing, and customer notification
Operators like RG Adventure send multiple "fully booked, please cancel" daily (3-6 bookings at a time)
quotes: "12GO28345868 12GO28344290 The ticket are fully booked please cancel." — Waeo Yuwari
"FAJNXQX - Dear Team, please kindly cancel and refund this customer because the boat will not operate due to Nyepi day" — Jasmine

title: Priority 1: Auto-Send Confirmation Requests
bullets: When a booking is created in the backoffice, auto-send "please confirm [booking_id]" to the operator's WhatsApp group
No human judgment needed — booking ID, operator group, and template are all deterministic

title: Priority 2: Auto-Send Ticket Attachment Requests
bullets: When a booking is confirmed but no ticket attached, auto-send request to operator group
Include timed escalation: reminder after 2 hours, flag for ops after 4 hours

title: Priority 3: Automated Follow-Up Escalation
callout: ~250+ messages eliminated, prevents multi-person chasing  |  Effort: Medium
bullets: If no operator response after X hours, auto-send follow-up
If still no response, escalate to ops for manual intervention
Prevents the pattern where 3-5 ops members independently chase the same booking

title: Priority 4: Operator Cancellation Self-Service
callout: Reduces reactive ops work significantly  |  Effort: Medium-High
bullets: Provide operators with a portal or bot to submit "fully booked" / "bad weather" / "cancel request"
Auto-updates backoffice and triggers customer notifications
Eliminates manual bridging of every operator cancellation message

title: Priority 5: Booking Thread Visibility Dashboard
callout: Prevents duplicate follow-ups, improves coordination  |  Effort: Medium
bullets: Dashboard showing: who last messaged, when, operator response status
Solves the root cause of 4-5 people chasing the same booking
Currently ops members have zero visibility into colleague activity

title: Priority 6: Zendesk-to-WhatsApp Integration
callout: Reduces complaint relay time  |  Effort: High
bullets: Auto-extract booking ID and complaint summary from Zendesk tickets
Draft or send message to appropriate operator WhatsApp group
Eliminates manual copy-paste workflow for complaint relay

title: Operator Responsiveness Scorecard
bullets: Based on ops:operator message ratio — higher ratio means more chasing by ops

rows: On time bus, Arab travel/Ticket Hub, West bus
Hepton Tours, Myticket, AB Maritime, Trip Egypt, Laos Group Tour
Stef Lanka, Wijaya Buyuk, Family Service, Otobeas
RG Adventure, Mekong Transfer, Atoll Transfer

title: Daily Workload Patterns
callout: No quiet days — sustained high volume 7 days a week
bullets: Peak day: 435 messages (2026-02-09 and 2026-03-05)
Lowest day: 249 messages (2026-02-20)

title: Key Findings Summary

bullets.text: 37% of ops outbound = confirmations + ticket requests
Unresponsive operators are the #1 time sink
sub_bullets: Worst cases: 12 ops messages, 0 replies, 10+ days

bullets.text: Multiple ops members chase the same booking
sub_bullets: Lack of thread visibility causes duplicate work
Ops-initiated (customer requests) and operator-initiated (capacity/weather) need different solutions

bullets: 80 WhatsApp groups create severe context-switching — top ops members juggle 20-33 groups daily
No proactive alerting — ops must manually check backoffice to know which bookings need action
